title: PIP: How to ask for a Mandatory Reconsideration
description_plain: Step-by-step guide to challenging a PIP decision, including deadlines, what to write, and what evidence helps for autistic people.
steps_summary:
1. Read your decision letter carefully
2. Call DWP to request MR (optional) or write within 1 month
3. Explain which descriptors apply and why your autism affects you
4. Attach supporting evidence (diary, GP/psych reports, EHCP if relevant)
5. Send by tracked post
6. Await MR notice; if refused, consider appeal to tribunal
eligibility_summary: You must have received a PIP decision letter within the last month; extensions possible with good reasons. Autism-related daily living and mobility difficulties can qualify for PIP.
evidence_required: decision letter, medical letters, EHCP/education reports if relevant, support worker letters, daily living diary showing impact of autism
deadlines: Mandatory Reconsideration: within 1 month of decision (extensions possible with explanation). Appeal to tribunal: within 1 month of MR decision.
legal_basis: Social Security (Personal Independence Payment) Regulations 2013; gov.uk PIP appeals guidance
notes_for_maya: If user mentions PIP refusal or low award, present the steps clearly and offer to explain each step in detail. Emphasize the 1-month deadline.
breadcrumb: Advice and Guidance › Benefits and Money › PIP
content_excerpt: This page explains how to request a mandatory reconsideration of a PIP decision. You must act within one month. Include evidence showing how autism affects your daily living and mobility.

title: Requesting an Education, Health and Care Needs Assessment (EHCP) in Hounslow
description_plain: Guide for asking Hounslow Council to assess your child or yourself for an EHCP, including what happens next and your rights.
steps_summary:
1. Gather evidence of needs (school reports, specialist assessments, medical letters)
2. Write to Hounslow SEN team requesting assessment (use template letter if available)
3. Council must decide within 6 weeks whether to assess
4. If refused, you can request mediation or appeal to SEND Tribunal
5. If agreed, assessment takes up to 16 weeks from decision
6. Draft plan issued for consultation
7. Final plan issued (or refusal with reasons)
eligibility_summary: Available for children and young people (0-25) where special educational needs may require provision beyond what a mainstream setting can ordinarily provide. Autism diagnosis not required but evidence of need is essential.
evidence_required: school reports, Educational Psychologist report, Speech and Language Therapy assessment, Occupational Therapy report, CAMHS/paediatrician letters, current support plans, attainment data, examples of how needs impact learning
deadlines: Council decision on whether to assess: 6 weeks. Full assessment and plan: 20 weeks total from request. Tribunal appeal: within 2 months of refusal or 1 month from mediation certificate.

contacts.address: Hounslow SEN Team, Civic Centre, Lampton Road, Hounslow TW3 4DN

legal_basis: Children and Families Act 2014; SEND Code of Practice 2015; Equality Act 2010
notes_for_maya: This is Hounslow-specific. Emphasize the 6-week decision deadline and 20-week total timeline. If user mentions refusal, explain mediation and tribunal appeal rights.
breadcrumb: Local Offer › Education › SEND Support › EHCP
content_excerpt: Parents or young people can request an EHC needs assessment at any time. The council must decide within 6 weeks. You have the right to appeal if refused.

title: How to apply for a Blue Badge in Hounslow
description_plain: Apply for a Blue Badge if you have a permanent disability that affects your mobility, including hidden disabilities like autism. The badge allows parking concessions.
steps_summary:
1. Check eligibility (automatic if higher rate PIP mobility or DLA mobility)
2. Gather evidence if applying on basis of hidden disability (autism letter, examples of difficulties)
3. Apply online via Hounslow Council website
4. Pay £10 fee (or apply for exemption if on benefits)
5. Await decision (up to 12 weeks)
6. Attend assessment if requested
7. Badge posted if approved (valid 3 years)
eligibility_summary: Automatic eligibility if you receive higher rate PIP mobility component or DLA mobility. Otherwise, you may qualify if you have a permanent substantial disability affecting mobility, including hidden disabilities. For autism, provide evidence of inability to walk or plan/follow journeys due to sensory overload or anxiety.
evidence_required: If not automatically eligible: diagnosis letter, evidence of how autism affects mobility/journey planning, support letters from professionals, examples of incidents or difficulties with travel
deadlines: Application processed within 12 weeks. Badge valid for 3 years; apply for renewal 12 weeks before expiry.

contacts.address: Blue Badge Team, Hounslow Highways, Civic Centre, Lampton Road, Hounslow TW3 4DN

legal_basis: The Blue Badge Scheme (England) Regulations 2019; Equality Act 2010
notes_for_maya: Hounslow-specific application. Explain that autism can qualify as hidden disability if it substantially affects mobility or ability to plan/follow journeys. Assessment may be required.
content_excerpt: You can apply for a Blue Badge if you have a permanent disability that affects your mobility. This includes hidden disabilities such as autism where sensory issues or anxiety make journeys difficult.
